description: Extended axis (sensory_axis_descriptors.json v1.2) to parser canonical axis mapping. Parser operates on 10 canonical axes; extended axes provide richer vocabulary for data annotation and future axis expansion.

clarity.note: Perceptual sharpness or diffusion. High clarity → light=Crystal/Clear, rendering=4K-like. Low clarity → light=Diffuse, rendering=Film-like.

finish.note: Temporal tail of a sensory experience (lingering, sharp cutoff, fading). Maps to time axis as duration/finish quality.

space.note: Perceived spatial scale or containment (expansive, enclosed, open). Maps to atmosphere as spatial quality.

shape.note: Perceived geometric quality (round, angular, layered). Round/soft → texture. Sharp/geometric → rendering.

edge.note: Perceptual boundary sharpness. Hard edge → texture=Hard/Sharp, light=Metallic. Soft edge → texture=Soft, light=Diffuse.

moisture.note: Perceived wetness or dryness. Wet → texture=Moist, organic_mineral=Vegetal/Aquatic. Dry → texture=Dry/Crisp, atmosphere=Arid.

balance.note: Compositional equilibrium or weight distribution. Heavy/dense → density=Dense. Light/airy → density=Light, atmosphere=Airy.

usage_note: Extended axes are used in vocabulary/sensory_axis_descriptors.json for data annotation and ontology design. They do not directly appear in ParserOutput. When writing sensory_objects.jsonl, use canonical axes only.
